config.rationale: Experiment 8/30 — first dedicated SGLang latency-optimization run. The SGLang baseline (4eb71ad47b0b) hit 155.8ms TTFT p95 — 3.5× worse than vLLM's 44.5ms latency leader — primarily because it was launched with --disable-radix-cache, defeating SGLang's biggest TTFT lever. Goal here is to close that gap by stacking SGLang's natural latency strengths. Per Rule 10, NOT retrying NEXTN (failed 2x: healthcheck_timeout). Per Rule 4 (latency goal): TP=1 (only 1 GPU available), enforce_eager=false (CUDA graphs help decode latency), modest batching (max_running_requests=128, max_prefill_tokens=8192) to avoid head-of-line blocking that inflates TTFT. Key changes vs SGLang baseline: (1) prefix caching ON (radix cache enabled, dropping --disable-radix-cache) — single biggest TTFT win for benchmarks with shared prompt prefixes; (2) scheduling_policy=lpm — explicitly designed to exploit radix cache by routing requests with overlapping prefixes together, further dropping prefill cost; (3) chunked_prefill_size=4096 — keeps each prefill chunk small so a queued prefill can't monopolize the GPU and starve a fresh decode, directly cutting TTFT p95 tail; (4) num_continuous_decode_steps=2 — halves scheduler invocation overhead per decode token, helping TPOT and indirectly TTFT under contention; (5) max_prefill_tokens=8192 caps a single prefill batch so big prompts get sliced. mem_fraction_static=0.85 (no NEXTN, so no auto-bump needed); max_model_len held at 65536 for apples-to-apples vs all prior runs and zero OOM risk on 80GB. dtype/kv_cache_dtype=auto, no quantization — clean latency-focused config without confounders. Reasoning + tool parsers preserved so smoke tests pass. If TTFT drops below ~80ms this becomes a credible SGLang latency contender; if not, next SGLang latency run will try chunked_prefill_size=2048 + lower max_running_requests=64.

smoke_tests.json_mode_detail: No content in response (reasoning model may not support JSON mode)

llm_commentary: SGLang latency run barely moved the needle — TTFT p95 dropped from 155.8ms to 153.4ms (~1.5%) despite enabling radix cache, lpm scheduling, and chunked prefill, and throughput at 5013 tok/s is ~30% below the vLLM Pareto front. It does not place on any leaderboard: best_latency remains 6c6509c4d045 (44.5ms), best_throughput remains 94f131fd3918 (7142), and best_balanced is dominated by three vLLM configs. The hypothesis that radix cache alone would close the 3.5× TTFT gap is falsified; SGLang appears structurally behind vLLM on this workload at TP=1.
docker_command: docker run --name bench-sglang-58d4bb8aeaf0 --gpus all --shm-size 16g --network host -v /root/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface -d lmsysorg/sglang:latest python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --tp-size 1 --dtype auto --enable-metrics --mem-fraction-static 0.85 --context-length 65536 --max-running-requests 128 --max-prefill-tokens 8192 --schedule-policy lpm --chunked-prefill-size 4096 --num-continuous-decode-steps 2 --reasoning-parser qwen3 --tool-call-parser qwen3_coder
